pages.title: About Me – Jeremy Corbyn MP
pages.text_excerpt: About Me – Jeremy Corbyn MP Home About Me News Help & Advice Contact Vacancies Privacy Policy Home About Me News Help & Advice Contact Vacancies Privacy Policy About Me I am honoured to have been re-elected to parliament in 2024. Islington North is my home, and the constituency I have devoted a great deal of my life to, as the elected represented since 1983. I have consistently campaigned on the things that matter to all of us, be they local or international. Decent housing is absolutely vital, and continues to be a priority, along with a variety of other important issues including social security benefits, education, immigration, health and much more. Human Rights issues that concern me and my constituents include anti-racism, anti-imperialism, the environment (transport), LGBT+ rights, opposition to nuclear weapons and military intervention, Trade Union policies and campaigning against miscarriages of justice. Significant roles I hold in parliament currently include holding that of Chair of 2 All Party Parliamentary Groups (APPG) Chagos Islands; Parliamentary Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament (CND), Vice President of the British Group of the Interparliamentary Union. Locally I am a trustee, or patron of a number of community organisations and I value these roles enormously, as they are invaluable opportunities to contribute to the ongoing existence of organisations that I see are doing excellent work in the borough. Aside from these specific roles I am in touch with people across the borough on a daily basis, when in the constituency. I am also Vice President of the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament, Acting Deputy President of Stop the War Coalition, and a Patron of the Palestine Solidarity Campaign I have been honoured with a variety of awards over the years; the most significant of these being the Sean McBride Peace Prize . pages.title: Help & Advice – Jeremy Corbyn MP
pages.text_excerpt: Help & Advice – Jeremy Corbyn MP Home About Me News Help & Advice Contact Vacancies Privacy Policy Home About Me News Help & Advice Contact Vacancies Privacy Policy Help & Advice Strict parliamentary code means I can only take up matters on your behalf if you live in Islington North, the constituency I represent in parliament . If you’re unsure whether you live in my constituency, please enter your postcode into the search bar in this link . If you have a personal issue, you need help with, my team and I may be able to help you. If your issue relates to Islington Council, in the first instance, please contact the Council directly or one of your local ward councillors. As the MP I am independent from the council and am not responsible for the decisions they make. Islington Council is responsible for: Housing allocation and housing repairs Rubbish collection, street cleaning and fly-tipping Council Tax, and some benefits relating to council tax and housing benefits Planning applications Noise nuisance, public health and Anti-social behaviour (in an emergency or where a crime has been committed always contact the emergency services on 999) Social Care and Children’s Services Allocation of school places, SEND You can contact Islington Council on 0207 527 4140 Click here for a directory of local support, services and things to do in Islington You can search for your local councillor here: https://democracy.islington.gov.uk/mgFindMember.aspx If you still want my help, please visit my contact page for more information on how to get in touch and arrange an appointment at one of my advice sessions. Please note, I receive a very large volume of enquiries, and these are dealt with in order of urgency. Due to the volume of correspondence I am in receipt of, I must prioritise helping my constituents with personal matters over responding on political issues. The following organisations can also be contacted for advice and support: The Citizens Advice Bureau provides free, confidential and independent advice to help people find their way forward. If you have a concern about a child or young person, the NSPCC helpline can be contacted for advice and guidance. The helpline is available twenty-four hours a day by telephoning 0808 800 5000 . Children and young people can contact ChildLine, which is available twenty-four hours a day, by telephoning 0800 1111 . All calls are free. If you have a concern about an adult at risk, you should contact the Local Authority Services for Adult Social Care. Use the following website to find the Local Authority Services for your area: https://www.gov.uk/find-local-council Adults and young people can contact the Samaritans for support. They are available twenty-four hours a day by telephoning 116 123 . All calls are free. The freephone, 24-hour National Domestic Abuse Helpline 0808 2000 247 If you or someone else is at immediate risk of harm or needs medical assistance, please contact the emergency services by telephoning 999. pages.title: Contact – Jeremy Corbyn MP
pages.text_excerpt: Contact – Jeremy Corbyn MP Home About Me News Help & Advice Contact Vacancies Privacy Policy Home About Me News Help & Advice Contact Vacancies Privacy Policy Contact I want to reassure you that my office is progressing casework and supporting constituents in Islington North during this challenging and difficult time. There is currently a very large volume of daily correspondence in relation to how the new government guidelines on Coronavirus are impacting daily life so please do bear this in mind when making an enquiry that is not time-sensitive or urgent. All advice sessions are being held over the phone until further notice. If you would like information on dates, times, and how to book an appointment, please get in touch with my local office. If you live in Islington North ( click here to check if you are unsure ) and wish to contact me about a personal matter (e.g. housing, benefits, immigration, etc): Email : corbynj@parliament.uk Telephone: 020 7561 7488 Monday, Tuesday, Thursdays between 10am-12md Write: Rt Hon Jeremy Corbyn MP, House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Please read these CONTACT GUIDELINES carefully: If you live in Islington North and have a policy query: Email: corbynj@parliament.uk and include your full postcode in the subject heading. Parliamentary protocol requires that MPs only assist people who live in the constituency they represent. Therefore, if you do not live in Islington North please contact your local MP. All other queries (including invitations) can be sent to: Email: corbynj@parliament.uk Telephone: 0207 219 3545 Write: Rt Hon Jeremy Corbyn MP, House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA You can also get advice and clarification on the links below: Government advice for the public Advice from Islington Council ACAS Employment Advice Evictions and protections for renters Rt Hon Jeremy Corbyn MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Bluesky Facebook Instagram X Copyright © 2026 Rt Hon Jeremy Corbyn MP We use cookies to improve the visitor's experience.
